SEARCH
Package dev.jeka.core.api.function

Class JkRunnables

java.lang.Object
dev.jeka.core.api.function.JkRunnables
All Implemented Interfaces:
Runnable
public class JkRunnables extends Object implements Runnable
A mutable container for Runnable. From this object you can replace the underlying Runnable or chain it with other ones.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    JkRunnables
    append(Runnable... runnables)
    Adds the specified Runnables at the end of the execution chain.
    JkRunnables
    append(String name, Runnable runnable)
    Adds the specified Runnable at the end of the execution chain.
    JkRunnables
    appendIf(boolean condition, String name, Runnable runnable)
    Same as append(String, Runnable) but effective only the specified condition
    boolean
    contains(String runnableName)
    Returns true if the execution chain contains a runnable with the specified name.
    void
    fillWith(JkRunnables runnables)
    Replaces the current entries, log, and task suffix of this JkRunnables instance with the entries, log, and task suffix of the specified JkRunnables instance.
    Runnable
    getRunnable(int index)
    Retrieves the Runnable stored at the specified index in the execution chain.
    List<String>
    getRunnableNames()
    Returns the name of the Runnables, in the order of execution chain.
    int
    getSize()
    Returns the total number of entries in the execution chain.
    JkRunnables
    insertAfter(String name, String afterRunnableName, Runnable runnable)
    Inserts the specified runnable in the execution chain, just after another one.
    JkRunnables
    insertBefore(String name, String beforeRunnableName, Runnable runnable)
    Inserts the specified runnable in the execution chain, just before another one.
    static JkRunnables
    of()
    Creates a JkRunnables delegating to a no-op runnable.
    JkRunnables
    remove(String runnableName)
    Removes the runnable having the specified name, from the execution list.
    JkRunnables
    replaceOrAppend(String name, Runnable runnable)
    Replaces the specified Runnable with the same name in the execution chain, or appends it if not found.
    JkRunnables
    replaceOrInsertBefore(String name, String beforeRunnableName, Runnable runnable)
    Replaces a Runnable with the same name in the execution chain if exists, otherwise inserts it before a specified runnable.
    void
    run()
    Executes the Runnables, in the order of the execution chain.
    JkRunnables
    setLogTasks(boolean log)
    Sets if each runnable execution should be wrapped in a log task.
    JkRunnables
    setTaskSuffix(String suffix)
    Sets the displayed suffix for the task.

    Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Method Details

    • of

      public static JkRunnables of()
      Creates a JkRunnables delegating to a no-op runnable.

    • append

      public JkRunnables append(String name, Runnable runnable)
      Adds the specified Runnable at the end of the execution chain.
      Parameters:
      name - The name referencing the runnable. The name can be used to remove the runnable form the chain or add others with place relative to this one.
      See Also:
      • remove(String)
      • insertBefore(String, String, Runnable)
      • insertAfter(String, String, Runnable)
      • contains(String)

    • append

      public JkRunnables append(Runnable... runnables)
      Adds the specified Runnables at the end of the execution chain.

    • appendIf

      public JkRunnables appendIf(boolean condition, String name, Runnable runnable)
      Same as append(String, Runnable) but effective only the specified condition

    • insertBefore

      public JkRunnables insertBefore(String name, String beforeRunnableName, Runnable runnable)
      Inserts the specified runnable in the execution chain, just before another one.
      Parameters:
      name - The name of the runnable to insert.
      beforeRunnableName - The name of the runnable that we must insert before.
      runnable - The runnable to insert.

    • replaceOrInsertBefore

      public JkRunnables replaceOrInsertBefore(String name, String beforeRunnableName, Runnable runnable)
      Replaces a Runnable with the same name in the execution chain if exists, otherwise inserts it before a specified runnable.
      Parameters:
      name - The name referencing the runnable.
      beforeRunnableName - The name of the runnable that the new runnable should be inserted before.
      runnable - The runnable to replace or insert.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the Runnable is null.

    • insertAfter

      public JkRunnables insertAfter(String name, String afterRunnableName, Runnable runnable)
      Inserts the specified runnable in the execution chain, just after another one.
      Parameters:
      name - The name of the runnable to insert.
      afterRunnableName - The name of the runnable that we must insert after.
      runnable - The runnable to insert.

    • remove

      public JkRunnables remove(String runnableName)
      Removes the runnable having the specified name, from the execution list.
      Parameters:
      runnableName - The name of the runnable to remove.

    • replaceOrAppend

      public JkRunnables replaceOrAppend(String name, Runnable runnable)
      Replaces the specified Runnable with the same name in the execution chain, or appends it if not found.
      Parameters:
      name - The name referencing the runnable.
      runnable - The Runnable to replace or append.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - If the Runnable is null.

    • getRunnableNames

      public List<String> getRunnableNames()
      Returns the name of the Runnables, in the order of execution chain.

    • contains

      public boolean contains(String runnableName)
      Returns true if the execution chain contains a runnable with the specified name.

    • setLogTasks

      public JkRunnables setLogTasks(boolean log)
      Sets if each runnable execution should be wrapped in a log task.

    • setTaskSuffix

      public JkRunnables setTaskSuffix(String suffix)
      Sets the displayed suffix for the task.

    • getSize

      public int getSize()
      Returns the total number of entries in the execution chain.

    • getRunnable

      public Runnable getRunnable(int index)
      Retrieves the Runnable stored at the specified index in the execution chain.

    • run

      public void run()
      Executes the Runnables, in the order of the execution chain.
      Specified by:
      run in interface Runnable

    • fillWith

      public void fillWith(JkRunnables runnables)
      Replaces the current entries, log, and task suffix of this JkRunnables instance with the entries, log, and task suffix of the specified JkRunnables instance.
      Parameters:
      runnables - The JkRunnables instance whose properties will replace the properties in this instance.
